CREATE TABLE events (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, event_name VARCHAR(100), event_time TIMESTAMP(3) WITH TIME ZONE);
SELECT event_name, event_time FROM events WHERE event_time >= '2023-10-01 00:00:00+00' AND event_time < '2023-10-02 00:00:00+00';
CREATE TABLE participants (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, event_id INT REFERENCES events(id), participant_name VARCHAR(100), registration_time TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE);
INSERT INTO participants (event_id, participant_name, registration_time) VALUES (1, 'John Doe', '2023-09-30 16:00:00+00'), (2, 'Jane Smith', '2023-10-01 11:00:00+03');
SELECT e.event_name, p.participant_name FROM events e INNER JOIN participants p ON e.id = p.event_id WHERE e.event_time WITH TIME ZONE > '2023-10-01 00:00:00+00';
CREATE TABLE feedback (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, participant_id INT REFERENCES participants(id), feedback_time TIMESTAMP(6) WITH TIME ZONE, feedback_text TEXT);
INSERT INTO feedback (participant_id, feedback_time, feedback_text) VALUES (1, '2023-10-01 15:00:00+00', 'Great event!'), (2, '2023-10-01 16:00:00+03', 'Very informative.');

SELECT e.event_name, COUNT(f.id) as feedback_count FROM events e LEFT JOIN participants p ON e.id = p.event_id LEFT JOIN feedback f ON p.id = f.participant_id GROUP BY e.event_name HAVING COUNT(f.id) > 0;
SELECT event_name, event_time AT TIME ZONE 'Europe/Berlin' AS local_time FROM events;

CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW event_overview AS SELECT e.event_name, e.event_time, COUNT(p.id) AS participant_count FROM events e LEFT JOIN participants p ON e.id = p.event_id GROUP BY e.event_name, e.event_time;
SELECT * FROM event_overview WHERE event_time BETWEEN '2023-10-01 00:00:00+00' AND '2023-10-02 00:00:00+00';
